Title: Anthocyanin accumulation differences in European pears caused by Phytochrome-interacting factor 3 (PcPIF3) promoter mutations under UV-B

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Starkrimson; Red Bartlett; Red Sichou; Zaosu; Red Anjou
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Starkrimson; Red Bartlett; Red Sichou; Zaosu; Red Anjou
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit peel red coloration and nutritional value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes anthocyanin accumulation and pear skin red coloration; higher expression is associated with reduced late-stage color fading in 'Starkrimson' compared with 'Red Bartlett'
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in pear peel and a key mediator of UV-B-responsive color retention differences between red-skinned European pear cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PcMYB10 transcription by direct binding to its promoter; increases PcUFGT expression indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: UV-B radiation; promoter sequence variation affecting PcWRKY11 binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in 'Zaosu' pear peel; transient silencing/RNAi in 'Red Sichou' pear peel; anthocyanin quantification; qRT-PCR; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; promoter-GUS assays; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in late fruit development stages (75–105 DAFB) in 'Starkrimson' peel than in 'Red Bartlett'; induced under moderate UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; positively correlated with anthocyanin accumulation; increased under moderate UV-B and higher in late-stage 'Starkrimson' peel
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation intensity; natural light; late fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis of peel from 75 DAFB 'Starkrimson' and 'Red Bartlett' pears; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression/localization constructs; native PcPIF3 promoters from 'Starkrimson' and 'Red Bartlett' for reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessively high UV-B did not further increase PcPIF3 expression; no other growth penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving red pear peel coloration and anthocyanin retention under field UV-B; promoter engineering or allele selection of PcPIF3 regulatory region may enhance coloring stability
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcPIF3 expression positively correlates with anthocyanin accumulation differences between 'Starkrimson' and 'Red Bartlett' during late fruit development. 2. Transient overexpression of PcPIF3 increases red coloration, anthocyanin content, and PcMYB10/PcUFGT expression, whereas silencing has the opposite effect. 3. PcPIF3 directly binds the PcMYB10 promoter G-box and activates transcription. 4. PcPIF3 promoter activity in 'Starkrimson', but not 'Red Bartlett', is stimulated by UV-B due to promoter variation enabling stronger upstream activation by PcWRKY11.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PcPIF3 increased PcUFGT transcript levels, Y1H and dual-luciferase assays showed direct binding to PcMYB10 promoter but not PcUFGT promoter; cultivar difference is driven by promoter cis-element mutation rather than coding sequence evidence.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Starkrimson; Red Bartlett; Zaosu; Red Sichou; Red Anjou
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Starkrimson; Red Bartlett; Zaosu; Red Sichou; Red Anjou
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit peel red coloration and nutritional value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration by activating PcPIF3 expression under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: UV-B-responsive upstream activator that explains cultivar-specific anthocyanin accumulation differences through selective binding to a mutated W-BOX 1 in the 'Starkrimson' PcPIF3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PcPIF3 transcription by binding W-BOX elements; indirectly upregulates PcMYB10 and PcUFGT through PcPIF3
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible negative regulation under high UV-B not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome-guided candidate screening; expression analysis under fruit development and UV-B treatments; transient overexpression in 'Zaosu'; transient silencing in 'Red Sichou'; anthocyanin quantification; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; GUS staining/activity assay; EMSA; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 'Starkrimson' peel than 'Red Bartlett' at late stages; strongly induced by higher UV-B; expression trend similar to PcPIF3
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; induced by UV-B radiation; overexpression increases anthocyanin and downstream gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation intensity; natural light; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis of peel from 75 DAFB 'Starkrimson' and 'Red Bartlett' pears used to identify candidate WRKY regulators; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for effector/localization constructs; native PcPIF3 promoters from 'Starkrimson' and 'Red Bartlett' for reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under high UV-B, PcWRKY11 expression did not increase significantly beyond moderate UV-B, suggesting possible unresolved inhibitory control; no agronomic penalties reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter-aware breeding target for enhancing anthocyanin retention in red pears; best deployed with favorable PcPIF3 promoter alleles containing effective WRKY-binding sites
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcWRKY11 was identified from transcriptome and expression analyses as the UV-B-responsive upstream regulator most consistent with PcPIF3 expression. 2. Overexpression of PcWRKY11 increases peel anthocyanin, red coloration, and expression of PcPIF3, PcMYB10, and PcUFGT, whereas silencing decreases them. 3. PcWRKY11 directly binds the mutated W-BOX 1 in the 'Starkrimson' PcPIF3 promoter and also binds W-BOX 2, but strong cultivar-specific activation is conferred by the additional W-BOX 1. 4. The PcWRKY11-PcPIF3 module under UV-B explains anthocyanin accumulation differences between red-skinned European pear cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other WRKYs (PcWRKY17, PcWRKY40a, PcWRKY40b, PcWRKY53) were screened as candidates, but the paper centered functional validation on PcWRKY11; cultivar specificity arises from promoter cis-mutation in PcPIF3 rather than exclusive presence of PcWRKY11.
